perturbation.original_text: REVENUES 4.1 plan_b shall pay LICENSOR a share of its revenues as set forth in APPENDIX 2 ("REVENUES").
perturbation.changed_text: REVENUES 4.1 plan_b shall pay LICENSOR a share of its revenues based on mutual agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'as set forth in APPENDIX 2' to 'based on mutual agreement', the contract now lacks a specific revenue share agreement defined in Appendix 2, which creates an in-text contradiction, as any reference to revenues in the rest of the text is ambiguous and unenforceable without a clear agreement in the Appendix. Appendix 2 does define the Revenue shares, so this change makes the contract contradict itself
perturbation.contradicted_text: APPENDIX 2 1 CONTENT, SHARE & TERRITORY 1.1 Contract name (for internal plan_b-ware use): Data Call Technologies, Inc. CONTENT LICENSOR TERRITORY TERMINATION SHARE Top News Headlines Top Business Headlines Science/Health News Entertainment Headlines National Football League National Basketball Association National Hockey League Major League Baseball NCAA Football NCAA Men's Basketball Professional Golf Association NASCAR Latest Sports Lines 45% Worldwide 24 months Latest Sports Headlines Thought for Today Market Details World Financial Highlights Weather: Current Conditions 48-Hour Forecast 7-Day Forecast Weather Alerts Doppler Weather Radar
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that revenue share is 'as set forth in APPENDIX 2'. The changed text states it's 'based on mutual agreement'. This contradicts APPENDIX 2 1.1, which already defines specific revenue shares (e.g., '45% Worldwide'). By removing the reference to Appendix 2 and replacing it with 'mutual agreement,' the contract introduces ambiguity and contradicts the existing, clearly defined revenue shares in Appendix 2, making the contract unenforceable and creating a direct conflict between the two sections.

perturbation.original_text: LICENSOR grants plan_b for the term of this Agreement the right to produce, market and distribute Content to End Users (in the territory specified in appendix 2) through its own and its partner's platform.
perturbation.changed_text: LICENSOR grants plan_b for the term of this Agreement the right to produce, market and distribute Content to End Users through its own and its partner's platform.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(in the territory specified in appendix 2)', the scope of where plan_b can distribute the content is undefined. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because Appendix 2 explicitly defines the territory for distribution. Without this clarification, the contract is unclear and Appendix 2's specification is not linked. The change contradicts the original intention as later specified in APPENDIX 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: APPENDIX 2 1 CONTENT, SHARE & TERRITORY 1.1 Contract name (for internal plan_b-ware use): Data Call Technologies, Inc. CONTENT LICENSOR TERRITORY TERMINATION SHARE Top News Headlines Top Business Headlines Science/Health News Entertainment Headlines National Football League National Basketball Association National Hockey League Major League Baseball NCAA Football NCAA Men's Basketball Professional Golf Association NASCAR Latest Sports Lines 45% Worldwide 24 months Latest Sports Headlines Thought for Today Market Details World Financial Highlights Weather: Current Conditions 48-Hour Forecast 7-Day Forecast Weather Alerts Doppler Weather Radar
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that plan_b's distribution rights were limited to the territory in Appendix 2. The changed text removes this territorial limitation, creating a contradiction with Appendix 2, which explicitly defines the territory. The change creates a direct conflict because plan_b's distribution rights are now undefined, overriding the territory specified in Appendix 2, thus falling under the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: After termination of this contract, there shall be a sell-off period (defined in APPENDIX 2) following the date of termination of this contract.
perturbation.changed_text: After termination of this contract, there shall be a sell-off period following the date of termination of this contract.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(defined in APPENDIX 2)', the length and specific rules around the 'sell-off period' are no longer defined. This contradicts Appendix 2, which specifies that the sell-off period is 3 months after termination. Without reference to this definition in the main body of the agreement, Appendix 2 is not linked and it creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: APPENDIX 2 1 CONTENT, SHARE & TERRITORY 1.1 Contract name (for internal plan_b-ware use): Data Call Technologies, Inc. CONTENT LICENSOR TERRITORY TERMINATION SHARE Top News Headlines Top Business Headlines Science/Health News Entertainment Headlines National Football League National Basketball Association National Hockey League Major League Baseball NCAA Football NCAA Men's Basketball Professional Golf Association NASCAR Latest Sports Lines 45% Worldwide 24 months Latest Sports Headlines Thought for Today Market Details World Financial Highlights Weather: Current Conditions 48-Hour Forecast 7-Day Forecast Weather Alerts Doppler Weather Radar 2 TERMS 2.1 Contract start: 04-01-06 2.2 Contract end: 04-01-08 2.3 Commercial distribution possible from: 2.4 Sell-off period: 3 months after termination
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly defined the 'sell-off period' as specified in APPENDIX 2. The changed text removes this definition, thereby creating ambiguity about the sell-off period's duration and rules. This directly contradicts APPENDIX 2, which states the sell-off period is '3 months after termination'. Without the explicit link in the main text, there is no clear indication that the sell-off period is defined and there is a direct contradiction.
